reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
REASONING: My Q1 answer (9×8=72) is correct. Games 3, 4, 5, and 6 all confirm that immediate Round 1 volunteering with simple arithmetic problems is the optimal strategy — everyone escaped with maximum coins in all four consecutive cases. These are simple arithmetic problems with ~95%+ expected accuracy per player, yielding only ~14% personal death risk vs. 25% random death risk from inaction. The expected value of volunteering now (≈3.43 coins) far exceeds waiting (≈1.93 coins). Four consecutive successful immediate volunteers proves this is the dominant strategy.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
REASONING: My own Q2-B answer (49÷7=7) is correct. These are simple arithmetic problems where each player has ~98% accuracy, giving ~94% combined success probability. This beats the 75% survival odds from risking random elimination if everyone passes (as happened to me in Game 2). Four consecutive games (3-6) confirmed that volunteering with single-endorsed capsules works on simple math. Volunteering now while keeping all 4 coins is the optimal strategy.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q1
REASONING: Following the consistently successful pattern from Games 3, 4, 5, and 6 — verifying Q1-A in Round 1 has reliably prompted A to volunteer immediately, resulting in full-team escapes every time. My Q3 answer (20 ÷ 5 = 4) is certain. Spending 1 coin to add my endorsement to Q1 builds consensus and signals coordination readiness. This 4-game streak demonstrates this is the optimal coordination strategy: I verify Q1, A volunteers, everyone escapes in Round 1. If A doesn't volunteer this round, I will volunteer in Round 2 to prevent any random-death spiral.
